mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-rate periods are not significantly lower than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the threshold, or t-statistic for return difference is insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in consumer demand, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and produces negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when either series declines alone, or combined signal IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Positive returns do not materialize in recovery regimes, or the signal reverses direction during expansion phases with p-value greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: High inflation combined with restrictive monetary policy forces the central bank to maintain elevated rates, increasing recession risk and depressing equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when both conditions hold, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects robust economic expansion and stronger corporate profit fundamentals, which consistently support positive S&P 500 forward returns over the monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when GDPC1 growth exceeds trend.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payrolls additions signal robust labor market health and consumer spending power, triggering immediate positive market repricing over the next week.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 150k in a single month
failure_condition: The threshold signal produces negative returns in >55% of triggered cases, or fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in retail sales and real GDP confirms synchronized demand expansion, which reinforces earnings upgrade cycles and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform single-series signals, or positive return frequency drops below 50% when both series expand simultaneously.
